---
title: "Temp"
author: "Daniel Dominguez"
date: "2026-04-20"
output: html_document
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

STEP 3 of the pipeline (runs AFTER Streamflow.Rmd).

Purpose
-------
Pull daily USGS water temperature (parameter 00010) for every site inside the
AOI, aggregate to one value per (site, Date), then LEFT JOIN the streamflow
table produced in Streamflow.Rmd.

End-state of this file
----------------------
**`q_t`** — one row per (site_no, Date) with `water_temp_C` and `q_m3s`.
Saved to `daily_drivers_dir/q_t.parquet` for consumption by Catchments.Rmd.

```{r setup, message=TRUE, warning=TRUE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

# Shared paths, packages, helper functions
source("SRC/Functions_Paths.R")
```

```{r load-aoi}
# Load AOI from Boundaries.py
aoi  <- st_read(basin_shp, quiet = TRUE) %>% st_transform(4326)
bbox <- st_bbox(aoi)

# Illinois River Basin bbox fallback (HUC 0712 approximate)
bbox_illinois <- c(-93.5, 36.8, -87.5, 42.0)  # West, South, East, North

# Time window
start_date <- as.Date("1984-01-01")
end_date   <- as.Date("2026-04-01")
```

```{r temp-metadata}
# Water temperature metadata (00010)
meta_temp <- read_waterdata_ts_meta(
  parameter_code = "00010",
  bbox           = bbox_illinois,
  properties     = c("monitoring_location_id", "parameter_code",
                     "begin", "end", "time_series_id"),
  skipGeometry   = TRUE
)

meta_temp_clean <- meta_temp %>%
  filter(!is.na(begin), !is.na(end)) %>%
  mutate(begin = as.Date(begin), end = as.Date(end)) %>%
  group_by(monitoring_location_id) %>%
  summarise(
    parameter  = first(parameter_code),
    start_date = min(begin),
    end_date   = max(end),
    n_series   = n(),
    .groups    = "drop"
  )

write.csv(meta_temp_clean, "illinois_water_temp_metadata.csv", row.names = FALSE)

sites_df <- meta_temp_clean %>% distinct(monitoring_location_id)
```

```{r pull-temp}
# Download water temperature for every site.
# `download_temp(site_id)` lives in SRC/Functions_Paths.R.
all_temp_data <- purrr::map_dfr(sites_df$monitoring_location_id, download_temp)
```

```{r clean-temp}
# Identify all water temp columns (exclude the *_cd flag columns)
temp_cols <- grep("Wtemp$", names(all_temp_data), value = TRUE)

all_temp_clean <- all_temp_data %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = all_of(temp_cols),
    names_to  = "sensor",
    values_to = "temp_value"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(temp_value)) %>%
  group_by(monitoring_location_id, Date) %>%
  summarise(
    water_temp_C = mean(temp_value, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups      = "drop"
  )

if (!exists("temp_clean_out")) {
  temp_clean_out <- file.path(daily_drivers_dir, "illinois_water_temp_clean.csv")
}
write.csv(all_temp_clean, temp_clean_out, row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r normalize-site-key}
# NWIS stores site numbers as bare site_no. The waterdata API returns
# monitoring_location_id like "USGS-05576195". Strip the prefix so we can
# join directly onto q_obs$site_no.
temp_daily <- all_temp_clean %>%
  mutate(
    site_no = gsub("^USGS-", "", monitoring_location_id),
    Date    = as.Date(Date)
  ) %>%
  group_by(site_no, Date) %>%
  summarise(
    water_temp_C = ifelse(all(is.na(water_temp_C)), NA_real_,
                          mean(water_temp_C, na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(Date >= start_date, Date <= end_date)

if (any(duplicated(temp_daily[c("site_no", "Date")]))) {
  stop("temp_daily has duplicate (site_no, Date) rows — fix aggregation.")
}
```

```{r join-q}
# Bring in Q measurements from Streamflow.Rmd
q_in  <- file.path(daily_drivers_dir, "q_obs.parquet")
q_obs <- read_parquet(q_in) %>%
  mutate(Date = as.Date(Date)) %>%
  select(site_no, Date, q_m3s)

# LEFT JOIN: keep every temperature row, add Q where the same site has Q
q_t <- full_join(q_obs, temp_daily, by = c("site_no", "Date"))
```

```{r save-qt}
# End-state of this file: T joined with Q
q_t_out <- file.path(daily_drivers_dir, "q_t.parquet")
write_parquet(q_t, q_t_out)

cat("\nQ+T summary:\n")
print(q_t %>% summarise(
  n_rows           = n(),
  n_sites          = n_distinct(site_no),
  min_date         = min(Date, na.rm = TRUE),
  max_date         = max(Date, na.rm = TRUE),
  pct_rows_with_Q  = mean(!is.na(q_m3s)),
  pct_rows_with_T  = mean(!is.na(water_temp_C))
))
```
